title: Bone density loss patterns in murine models exposed to long-duration microgravity
abstract: This study investigates the effects of microgravity on Mus musculus with focus on the skeletal system. Using longitudinal phenotyping and imaging, we identify regulatory pathways altered during exposure and propose candidate biomarkers for future long-duration mission planning.

title: Transcriptomic response of Arabidopsis roots to spaceflight conditions on the ISS
abstract: Arabidopsis thaliana root tissue was profiled after long-duration ISS exposure to determine gene expression changes driven by microgravity. The analysis highlights altered auxin signaling and cell wall remodeling in the plant growth system.

title: Cardiovascular deconditioning markers in astronauts: a six-month longitudinal study
abstract: Human astronauts were monitored for cardiovascular adaptation during extended missions. The study identifies biomarkers linked to blood pressure regulation and cardiac output in response to microgravity stress.

title: Cosmic radiation-induced DNA damage in human lymphocytes aboard the ISS
abstract: This research measures DNA damage and repair responses in Homo sapiens lymphocytes exposed to cosmic radiation. Results point to immune system stress and potential long-term genomic instability challenges.

title: Skeletal muscle atrophy mechanisms under simulated microgravity in C. elegans
abstract: Caenorhabditis elegans muscle tissues were evaluated under simulated microgravity to identify pathways driving atrophy. Data reveal conserved regulation of muscular proteins and stress response genes.

title: Yeast cell cycle dynamics in low Earth orbit: an omics-driven analysis
abstract: Saccharomyces cerevisiae growth and cell cycle progress were tracked in low Earth orbit. The omics data reveal metabolic shifts and cellular metabolism adaptation to microgravity conditions.

title: Drosophila innate immunity response to spaceflight oxidative stress
abstract: Fruit fly immune genes were profiled after ISS exposure to assess oxidative stress response. The findings highlight conserved innate immune signatures and organism resilience to spaceflight conditions.

title: Plant gravitropism and auxin signaling pathways in microgravity environments
abstract: Auxin signaling and gravitropic growth responses were characterized in Arabidopsis thaliana under microgravity. Results show altered hormone dynamics in the plant growth system.

title: Hindlimb unloading rat model: gene expression in soleus muscle fibers
abstract: Rats in hindlimb unloading models show gene expression changes in soleus muscle fibers. The data link unloading stress to muscular adaptation and bone-muscle crosstalk.

title: Microbial biofilm formation on spacecraft surfaces under microgravity
abstract: Escherichia coli biofilm dynamics were monitored on spacecraft materials in microgravity. The study identifies microbial behavior changes and implications for planetary protection.
